task: Research question: the provided data describe a Great Britain power system in which a large amount of onshore wind is concentrated in Scotland while most demand sits in England, connected by a limited set of Scotland-England transmission links. Investigate how much wind energy is wasted (curtailed) in this system, identify what causes the curtailment, and determine whether that cause is genuinely causal rather than merely correlated. You decide how to model the system (e.g. with an optimal-power-flow tool such as PyPSA), how to quantify curtailment, and how to design experiments that isolate the mechanism. Beyond characterizing the baseline, you must design and run at least one nontrivial counterfactual/sensitivity experiment that intervenes on the system to test a specific hypothesis about the mechanism - and ideally one that pits competing explanations against each other (for example: can storage or demand-side flexibility substitute for transmission and relieve curtailment as effectively as building more lines?). Use the interventions to argue whether the transmission bottleneck is the dominant causal driver, state which of your conclusions are robust to modeling choices and which are artifacts, and report findings even when an intervention you expected to help does not. The scientific aim is a transparent, reproducible analysis of curtailment and its drivers in a renewable-rich, transmission-constrained grid.

data.name: buses.csv
data.description: Defines the buses (nodes) of the power system, including bus name, nominal voltage, and carrier type. This information forms the foundation of the grid topology.

data.name: links.csv
data.description: Describes transmission lines (or links), including source bus, target bus, nominal power capacity, line length, and carrier type. Used to simulate grid transmission capabilities and constraints.

data.name: demand.csv
data.description: Provides hourly active power demand (MW) at each bus for 168 hours (one week). This is the electricity demand time series that the system must satisfy.

data.name: generators.csv
data.description: Lists all generator units with attributes such as bus location, carrier type (e.g., wind, gas, nuclear), rated capacity, and marginal cost. Defines the generation resources of the system.

data.name: wind_cf.csv
data.description: Contains hourly wind capacity factors (0~1) for each bus, reflecting the temporal variability of wind resources. Used to calculate the maximum available output of wind turbines.

data.name: storage.csv
data.description: Describes the parameters of storage units, including bus location, type (e.g., pumped hydro), power capacity, energy capacity, and charge/discharge efficiency. Used to simulate the charging and discharging behavior of storage.
